primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: Mass spectrometry-based proteomic analysis of urinary EV (uEV) in men with benign and malignant prostate disease

In order to identify cancer-specific protein signatures, EV-enriched (Figure 5(a)) and protein-enriched (Figure 6(a)) urine samples, fractionated from men diagnosed with benign prostate hyperplasia (n = 12) were compared with those from prostate cancer patients (n = 12).

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison across disease stages, grades, or therapeutic response groups.
evidence: Matched urine samples were collected from prostate cancer patients prior to (n = 12) and 3 months after local treatment by radical prostatectomy or intensity-modulated radiation therapy (n = 10).
The composition of the EV-enriched (Figure 5(a)) and protein-enriched (Figure 6(a)) urinary proteome changed significantly after local prostate cancer treatment

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between distinct tissue or organ sources.
evidence: Finally, a comparative proteomic analysis with uEV from patients with bladder and renal cancer provided additional evidence of the selective enrichment of protein signatures in uEV reflecting their respective cancer tissues of origin.

attributes.agent: local treatment (radical prostatectomy or intensity-modulated radiation therapy)
evidence: Matched urine samples were collected from prostate cancer patients prior to (n = 12) and 3 months after local treatment by radical prostatectomy or intensity-modulated radiation therapy (n = 10).

control_arms: prior to treatment (as baseline for post-treatment)
replicate_hints: Technical replicates (n = 6) derived from a pool of cell-free urine
Three repeated runs per sample were performed.

evidence: In order to identify cancer-specific protein signatures, EV-enriched (Figure 5(a)) and protein-enriched (Figure 6(a)) urine samples, fractionated from men diagnosed with benign prostate hyperplasia (n = 12) were compared with those from prostate cancer patients (n = 12).
The composition of the EV-enriched (Figure 5(a)) and protein-enriched (Figure 6(a)) urinary proteome changed significantly after local prostate cancer treatment

Finally, a comparative proteomic analysis with uEV from patients with bladder and renal cancer provided additional evidence of the selective enrichment of protein signatures in uEV reflecting their respective cancer tissues of origin.

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly uses data dependent acquisition (DDA) and label-free quantification (LFQ) via the MaxLFQ algorithm in MaxQuant.
The study explicitly uses data dependent acquisition (DDA) and label-free quantification (LFQ) via the MaxLFQ algorithm in MaxQuant.
